iti79:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti79:2.1: “Monks, these three things lead to the falling away of a monk in training.
iti79:2.3:
There is the case where a monk in training enjoys activity,
delights in activity, is intent on his enjoyment of activity.
iti79:2.4: He enjoys chatter, delights in chatter, is intent on his enjoyment of chatter.
iti79:2.5: He enjoys sleep, delights in sleep, is intent on his enjoyment of sleep.
iti79:2.6: These are the three things that lead to the falling away of a monk in training.
iti79:3.1: “These three things lead to the non-falling away of a monk in training.
iti79:3.3:
There is the case where a monk in training doesn’t enjoy activity,
doesn’t delight in activity, isn’t intent on his enjoyment of activity.
iti79:3.4: He doesn’t enjoy chatter, doesn’t delight in chatter, isn’t intent on his enjoyment of chatter.
iti79:3.5: He doesn’t enjoy sleep, doesn’t delight in sleep, isn’t intent on his enjoyment of sleep.
iti79:3.6: These are the three things that lead to the non-falling away of a monk in training.”
iti79:5.1: So he should be a man of few duties,
